name: Firewall rule allows SSH (TCP/22) from the internet
description: A VPC firewall rule allows inbound TCP traffic on port 22 (SSH) from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0. This exposes every VM in the target network or tags to brute-force, credential stuffing, and exploitation of SSH vulnerabilities. Unrestricted SSH access is one of the most common initial access vectors in GCP environments.
remediation: Delete or restrict the firewall rule to known management CIDRs. Use Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP) for OS Login to tunnel SSH without a public firewall rule: gcloud compute ssh --tunnel-through-iap. If SSH must be exposed, use Cloud Armor and restrict source IPs to your NAT gateway.
